channel.title: backend on Patrick Armengol
channel.description: Recent content in backend on Patrick Armengol

item.title: Golang Learning Plan
item.description: Why Golang (yes, I think I’m going to call it golang because it makes it easier to search and because I associate the word go with the board game) is a nice language. It seems simple but capable. Looks like I should be able to use it to dive deeper into things like systems programming, devops, sre, concurrency-driven apps, and most importantly, backend web dev. Up until now I have been using python for basically anything and everything and I’m quite comfortable with it, but it often doesn’t really feel like the best tool for any job other than maybe data science/analysis.

item.title: Backend Learning Plan
item.description: Skills Internet Basics HTTP DNS how do browsers work? Python see Python Learning Plan Python Frameworks Django Flask FastAPI Litestar Golang see Golang Learning Plan Databases PostgreSQL SQLite MongoDB ORMs ACID transactions N + 1 problem database normalization data replication sharding strategies CAP theorem APIs REST JSON gRPC OpenAPI spec Authentication and Authorization basic auth cookies and sessions JSON web tokens OAuth (Google, GitHub) SAML auth libraries (passport, grant) password hashing (bcrypt) Web Security SSL/TLS HTTPS CSP CORS OWASP top 10 Caching client-side server-side CDN Testing integration testing unit testing functional testing CI/CD ?
